column1: # Subagent model/effort routing map — single source of truth.
# Claude subagent frontmatter declares a model TIER (haiku/sonnet/opus). Each
# runtime resolves that tier to its own model + effort here. The tier is the
# SSOT: to change a role's tier, edit the agent's frontmatter, not this table.
# Runtime effort support:
#   - Codex: native per-agent model + reasoning effort (both columns honored).
#   - Kiro:  native per-agent model only. The Q/Kiro agent schema has no
#            per-agent effort field; effort is a per-MODEL default applied once
#            via chat.modelDefaults at home prepare. kiro_model must be an ID
#            the /model service returns, else Kiro falls back to default + warns.
# Tier mapping is empirically tuned (gpt-5.6 luna/terra/sol measured on
# representative subagent tasks), NOT a mechanical opus->sol lift:
#   - haiku  -> luna/low     : local read/record roles; luna medium already
#                              scores 92-96 and is cheapest by 3-4x on local work.
#   - sonnet -> terra/medium : exploration/implement roles. Measured: on an
#                              exploration task terra used HALF luna's tokens at
#                              equal cost and 92 quality; sol added only +4 pts
#                              for 2x cost. Terra is the cost/quality sweet spot
#                              here — the "terra is Pareto-dominated" vendor
#                              benchmark did not hold for this workload.
#   - opus   -> sol/high     : deep-judgment roles (review, design) only, where
#                              failure cost is high and invocation frequency low,
#                              so sol's premium is immaterial.
# Columns (tab-separated): claude_tier  codex_model  codex_effort  kiro_model
# claude_tier "default" applies to any agent with no/unknown model: field.
haiku
sonnet
opus
default
